Texas Dietitian License Reciprocity & How to Apply

Already licensed elsewhere? Start with compact status, endorsement requirements, fees, and your likely transfer timeline.

Last verified 2026-02-25
No Interstate Compact

How to Transfer Your Dietitian License to Texas

There is no active interstate compact covering dietitian practice in Texas. To work in Texas, apply for licensure by endorsement through Dietitians Program.

Endorsement fee
$142
Processing time
2 to 4 Weeks
Fingerprinting
Required
Temporary license
Not listed
See endorsement steps Official board portal

Endorsement Application Steps

  1. Pass the Texas Jurisprudence Exam

    Before applying, you must take and pass the online Texas Jurisprudence Exam covering state laws. You will pay a $34 fee to the third-party provider.

  2. Apply Online via TDLR

    Create an account in the TDLR Online Licensing System. Submit your application for a new Dietitian License and pay the $108 fee.

  3. Pass the DPS Background Check

    TDLR conducts a criminal background check through the Texas Department of Public Safety (DPS). You will be sent a 'FAST Pass' to complete fingerprinting.

Quick Facts

Total fee
$142
Processing time
2 to 4 Weeks
Renewal fee
$90
Renewal cycle
Every 2 years + 12 CE hours

Required Documents

  • Copy of current CDR registration card: Must be in active status.
  • Texas Jurisprudence Exam Certificate: Proof of successful completion.

Board Contact

Board
Dietitians Program
Department
Texas Department of Licensing and Regulation (TDLR)
Phone
(800) 803-9202
Email
cs.dietitians@tdlr.texas.gov

Visit official board portal

Renewal

Renewal fee is $90. Renewal cycle: Every 2 years + 12 CE hours.

Texas Dietitian Reciprocity FAQ

How much does a Texas dietitian license cost?

The initial license application fee is $108, paid to TDLR. You must also pay a $34 fee to a third-party vendor to take the mandatory Texas Jurisprudence Examination, bringing the total to $142.

What is the Texas Jurisprudence Exam for Dietitians?

It is a mandatory, online examination covering state laws, rules, and regulations relating to the practice of dietetics in Texas. All initial applicants must pass it.

Does Texas have reciprocity for dietitians?

No interstate compact currently covers dietitian practice in Texas. To work in Texas, apply for licensure by endorsement through the state board.

How long does it take to transfer my dietitian license to Texas?

Most endorsement applications take 2 to 4 Weeks. If you qualify for an active compact privilege, there may be no separate wait to start practicing.

Can I start working in Texas while my endorsement application is processing?

Most applicants need to wait for full approval before starting work because the state does not clearly offer a temporary or provisional credential.
